title: Make retained report changes atomic and durable
description: Commit retained generation, Restatement, deletion, idempotency, lifecycle evidence, and storage evolution as complete attributable outcomes or not at all.
target: For 100% of injected failure, cancellation, and crash points, restart inspection observes either the complete Retain, Restate, Delete, or migration outcome exactly once or the unchanged pre-operation state; zero partial report payloads, dangling Restatement links, duplicate logical identities, missing Deletion Events, or consumed failed-request identities occur.
load_condition: Every retained-state write at each boundary before validation, producer reads, report calculation, transaction start, each durable write, commit, result delivery, restart, and retry.
measurement: Deterministic fault injection, process termination, disk-full and busy cases, restart integrity inspection, idempotent replay, changed-input conflict, and complete-state fingerprint comparisons.
rationale: An immutable report history is useful only when interruptions cannot produce ambiguous or half-written evidence.
